build.notes: Build completed successfully with only performance warnings about bundle size

lint.notes: No lint errors in TestimonialsCarousel files. Only warnings about experimental WordPress APIs which are acceptable project-wide

registration.notes: block.json valid, all required files present, apiVersion 3, attributes properly defined

testPageVerification.notes: Test page contains 6 block instances with <!-- wp:takt/testimonials-carousel --> markers

attributes.notes: All attributes defined in block.json with correct types and defaults

accessibility.notes: Close button has proper aria-label and is focusable, but modal duplication issue affects accessibility

markupDuplication.notes: Critical: Modal is duplicated 6 times instead of rendered once globally

tsxPhpSync.notes: TSX correctly does not render the modal (PHP-only). Main carousel structure matches between TSX and PHP

javascriptFunctionality.notes: JavaScript correctly targets .testimonial-video-close class, close button has both classes (testimonial-video-close for JS, modal-close-btn for styling)

specCompliance.notes: Test page variations match spec requirements

issues.description: Modal is rendered 6 times (once per block instance) instead of once globally. The static $testimonial_modal_rendered flag is not working across multiple block renders.
issues.technicalDetail: WordPress's block render system includes the template file within a function scope for each block instance. Static variables are scoped to the function, not globally, so each render creates a new scope and the static variable resets. Found 6 .testimonial-video-modal elements on page with 6 block instances.
issues.impact: Performance: 6x DOM nodes. Potential JS issues: querySelector('.testimonial-video-close') will only target the first modal's close button, which may cause modal functionality to break depending on DOM order.

issues.description: Close button positioning changed from overlapping corner to above video area, but class change may need verification for focus indicator visibility on dark backdrop
issues.technicalDetail: New .modal-close-btn class uses white border and white text (line 97-99) which provides good contrast on dark backdrop. However, focus indicator styles should be verified to ensure visibility. The carousel-nav-btn class had specific focus styles that may need to be replicated for modal-close-btn.
issues.impact: Keyboard users may have difficulty seeing focus state on the close button against the dark backdrop

passedChecks: Build compiles without errors (only bundle size warnings)
All block files present (block.json, index.tsx, TestimonialsCarousel.tsx, TestimonialsCarousel.php)
Test page has 6 block instances with proper <!-- wp: --> markers
All attributes defined with correct types and defaults
TSX does not render modal (PHP-only as intended)
Close button has both classes: testimonial-video-close (JS target) and modal-close-btn (CSS styling)
Close button has proper aria-label='Close video'
Close button positioned above video area (-top-12, md:-top-14)
modal-close-btn class exists in swiper.css with white border and icon for contrast
Carousel navigation buttons use separate carousel-nav-btn class
No lint errors in block files (only acceptable experimental API warnings)
Carousel renders with proper data attributes for Swiper configuration
Video buttons present on cards with videos

modalRendering.expected: 1 modal rendered globally, shared by all block instances

modalCloseBtn.styling: White border, white icon, gradient hover, isolated from carousel-nav-btn

carouselNavBtn.styling: Dark mode aware border/text, gradient hover

javascriptTargeting.status: Correct - uses querySelector once globally, not per-block
